Problem Area: Port City Disconnect,"Demaritimisation: Locals feel ""sea-blindness"";" the port is a closed fortress
Sociological Intervention (The Fix);: ""Hydro-Social"""" Urban Planning: Design cities where the """"wet"""" and """"dry"""" mix. Open port interfaces
column5: and festivals that celebrate contemporary maritime labor

Problem Area: Workforce Aging,"Identity Crisis: Youth perceive maritime jobs as ""dirty, dangerous, and dull"" (3D) or purely extractive.","Rebranding ""People of the Sea"": Use Janiszewski's category to elevate the status of maritime workers. Frame them as ""Climate Guardians"" (e.g., wind techs, sustainable fishers) to appeal to Gen Z values.";

Gentrification,"Blue Displacement: ""Blue Growth"" tourism pushes out the original working class (fishers), destroying the authentic culture tourists came for.","Blue Justice Zoning: Protect ""working waterfronts"" legally. Ensure affordable housing for maritime workers to keep the ""living memory"" of the coast alive.";

Digital Divide,"Technological Alienation: Traditional fishers feel replaced by algorithms and drones, leading to non-compliance.","Participatory Digitalization: Co-design apps with fishers. Treat their ""tacit knowledge"" (generations of experience) as data that validates the AI, giving them ownership of the transition.";
